top_weaknesses.issue: H2s are too generic ('Why Raw Volume Decays', 'The Flywheel Has to Be Operational', 'Data Rights Are Part of the Moat') — could fit any AI essay, no anchoring to a concrete domain or named case
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen H2s: 'Why More Data Stops Helping (And Starts Hurting)', 'The Moat Is a Feedback Loop, Not a Warehouse', 'Data Rights: Procurement-Proof or It Doesn't Count'

top_weaknesses.issue: VC-share stat (61%) is off-topic for a data-moat essay AND uses a low-trust source; distracts from the core claim and triggers diligence reflex
top_weaknesses.fix: Either drop the stat (this essay is not about funding) or cite a primary source

top_weaknesses.issue: Named-but-vague references ('a16z's essay on...', 'Insignia Business Review's analysis...') read as citation-scented synthesis without an operator anecdote to anchor
top_weaknesses.fix: Add a 2-3 sentence concrete case (legal intake or freight brokerage agent: what data, what loop, what metric moved)

top_weaknesses.issue: No scannable framework or checklist; closing paragraph is template-shaped slogan
top_weaknesses.fix: Add a 4-6 bullet 'hoard vs moat' checklist, sharpen the close to a single non-obvious decision rule

consensus_summary: The flywheel/warehouse framing is genuinely shareable and the operational-feedback section is the strongest, but the H2 outline is too generic to survive a skim and the off-topic VC stat plus vague citations weaken credibility. Sharpen H2s, drop or source the stat, and add one concrete operator example.
